Methods & Experiments
Hypotheses (collapsed)
TitleLeverDirConfidence
Hypoglycemia burden present — prioritize low preventionbasal,ICR,ISF,timing/modelriskMedium
Overnight downward drift suggests basal too strongbasaltoo strongMedium
Post-breakfast highs suggest ICR too weakICRtoo weakMedium
Post-lunch highs suggest ICR too weakICRtoo weakMedium
Post-dinner highs suggest ICR too weakICRtoo weakMedium
Most actionable
Corrections: ISF ±10–20% — midday(11-13) | iob 0.5-1.5→ISF stronger ~20%Med
Corrections: ISF ±10–20% — other | iob>1.5→ISF stronger ~20%Med

AGP (14‑day)

Shaded bands: 25–75% (inner) and 5–95% (outer). Target band: 70–180 mg/dL.

How to read

  • Dark line = median; inner band = 25–75%; outer band = 5–95%.
  • Green zone marks 70–180 mg/dL target.
  • Look for repeat median excursions and wide bands (variability).

Key takeaways (auto‑generated)

  • No strong median excursions detected; treat meal‑specific lenses as primary.

GRI components

Purple = LBGI (hypo risk), Orange = HBGI (hyper risk).

School Meal Lens

WindowLead binnStart BG (med/IQR)ΔPeak (med)T→180 (med)Start trend%>180Median peak
Breakfast 7:00–8:00pre0-46122.5/37272mflat (50%)33.3%149.5
Breakfast 7:00–8:00pre10-193132/12263mfalling (67%)33.3%160
Breakfast 7:00–8:00pre5-91123/0613mflat (100%)100%184
Lunch 11:20–12:10pre>=201159/0615mflat (100%)100%220
T→180 = return to 180 mg/dL after first >180 crossing within 4h. “≤180” = never >180; “>4h” = no return within 4h.

How to read

  • Lead bin: dosing timing relative to meal (e.g., pre≥20, pre0–4, post).
  • ΔPeak: median peak minus start; lower is better if peaks are excessive.
  • T→180: time to return ≤180 after first >180 within 4h.

Key takeaways (auto‑generated)

  • Breakfast: 59% >180; median peak ~184
  • Lunch: 60% >180; median peak ~221
  • Dinner: 76% >180; median peak ~209

Dinner Lens

  • Dinner %>180: 76%
  • Median peak: 209
  • T→180 (med): 4m
Directional only if n is small; use with School Meal Lens for daytime context.

Experiments v1

Last update: 2026-02-16T23:54:00Z
  • Lunch +10 min pre-bolus (Weekdays) — Weekdays 11:20–12:10
    Goal: Trim lunch TAR below 60% and cut peaks >280 mg/dL · Metric: Lunch 4h TAR, % predicted ≤ suspend, CGM 120–240 min slope · Owner: Dan · Status: logging
    Log at least 5 lunches with visible dosing; aim +10 min default, escalate to +20 if trend >200 mg/dL after 60 min.
  • Corrections ISF sweep (+10%) — Anytime corrections (IOB <1.5, no carbs ±4h)
    Goal: Lift median 2h drop to ≥35 mg/dL and reduce ineffective cells · Metric: Corrections lens @120m (n, % ineffective, median drop) · Owner: Dan · Status: planned
    Pilot at +10% on clean corrections when predicted ≤ suspend <20%; pause if lows or comm errors spike.
  • ISF +10% — 14:00–16:30 CT (10 days) — 14:00–16:30 (America/Chicago)
    Goal: Assess daytime ISF bias and correction effectiveness in mid‑afternoon while lunch timing is tested separately. · Metric: ISF Bias @120m (Observed − Expected), n, 95% CI; % ineffective @2h; constraint chips · Owner: Dan · Status: running
    Track clean manual corrections ≥0.3 U; no carbs/exercise ±4h; avoid stacking. Flag ready-to-call when n≥6 and constraints low.
Past
  • Breakfast +5g add-on (Jan cohort) — Breakfast 6:30–8:30
    Goal: Test if 5g add-on offsets cereal under-counts · Metric: Breakfast peaks & 90–150 min >180 · Owner: Dan · Status: complete
    No material change; reverted to exact carb logging.

Scenario Cards

Corrections: ISF ±10–20% — midday(11-13) | iob 0.5-1.5

Linked hypothesis: Corrections often ineffective → ISF may be too weak
midday(11-13) | iob 0.5-1.5
Lever(s): ISF · Direction: ISF stronger → larger drops; weaker → smaller drops · Confidence: Low
Pred ≤ suspend19%AB cadence33%Max basal hits0%

Quick take: Pred ≤ suspend pressure is moderate and AB cadence is high, so ISF stronger ~20% stays the lead lever because it cleans up stubborn corrections fastest while max basal headroom stays open for timing/ISF moves.

  1. ISF stronger ~20% — ↑↑ 2–3h drops; ↓ 90–180 min TAR; avoid stacking Med
  2. ISF stronger ~10% — ↑ 2–3h drops; ↓ TAR; monitor predicted lows Med
  3. As-is (baseline) — for comparison Med
  1. ISF stronger ~20% — ↑↑ 2–3h drops; ↓ 90–180 min TAR; avoid stacking Med
  2. ISF stronger ~10% — ↑ 2–3h drops; ↓ TAR; monitor predicted lows Med
  3. As-is (baseline) — for comparison Med
Why this ranking
  • n=4, 2h drop median -78 mg/dL; 3h -36
  • Ineffective at 2h (<30 mg/dL drop): 75.0%
Risks
  • Avoid back-to-back corrections within DIA
  • Higher ISF increases drop magnitude; watch lows 2–3h post-bolus
Validation
  • Sandbox 1–2 clean corrections in this band: observe 2h/3h drops and lows; adjust hypothesis direction only (no numeric changes).

Corrections: ISF ±10–20% — other | iob>1.5

Linked hypothesis: Corrections often ineffective → ISF may be too weak
other | iob>1.5
Lever(s): ISF · Direction: ISF stronger → larger drops; weaker → smaller drops · Confidence: Low
Pred ≤ suspend19%AB cadence33%Max basal hits0%

Quick take: Pred ≤ suspend pressure is moderate and AB cadence is high, so ISF stronger ~20% stays the lead lever because it cleans up stubborn corrections fastest while max basal headroom stays open for timing/ISF moves.

  1. ISF stronger ~20% — ↑↑ 2–3h drops; ↓ 90–180 min TAR; avoid stacking Med
  2. ISF stronger ~10% — ↑ 2–3h drops; ↓ TAR; monitor predicted lows Med
  3. As-is (baseline) — for comparison Med
  1. ISF stronger ~20% — ↑↑ 2–3h drops; ↓ 90–180 min TAR; avoid stacking Med
  2. ISF stronger ~10% — ↑ 2–3h drops; ↓ TAR; monitor predicted lows Med
  3. As-is (baseline) — for comparison Med
Why this ranking
  • n=5, 2h drop median 102 mg/dL; 3h 125
  • Ineffective at 2h (<30 mg/dL drop): 0.0%
Risks
  • Avoid back-to-back corrections within DIA
  • Higher ISF increases drop magnitude; watch lows 2–3h post-bolus
Validation
  • Sandbox 1–2 clean corrections in this band: observe 2h/3h drops and lows; adjust hypothesis direction only (no numeric changes).
Legend: Chips show Pred ≤ suspend (green <10%, amber 10–25%, red ≥25%), AB cadence (<15%, 15–30%, >30%), and Max basal hits (0%, 0–5%, ≥5%). Risk chips align with the same color scale.

Corrections Lens

2h · 3h SVG
Blue = stronger drops, white = neutral, orange/red = weak or rising corrections. Overlay text shows n | %ineff2h.

How to read

  • Columns are dayparts; rows are IOB bands at the time of correction.
  • Cell color encodes median BG change after the correction (bluer = larger drop).
  • Text shows sample size and 2h ineffectiveness (n | %ineff2h; ineffective = <20 mg/dL drop).

Key takeaways (auto‑generated)

  • Some cells have small n; treat those cells as directional only.

Corrections @120m (ISF Bias)

ContextnMedian 2h ΔObserved drop/U @120mExpected ISFISF Bias
midday(11-13) | iob 0.5-1.54-78-260145ISF weaker (-1.79×)
other | iob<0.52-12-62n/an/a
other | iob>1.55102216n/an/a
overnight(0-4) | iob 0.5-1.51-5-12142ISF weaker (-0.09×)
overnight(0-4) | iob>1.5177257142ISF stronger (1.81×)
Bias = Observed ÷ Expected ISF by daypart. Treat small‑n rows as directional only.
